rationale: The page contains detailed information on three distinct scientific and technological topics: Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology, Hyperloop Mobility Systems, and the Deep Ocean Mission. These are high-value topics for generating questions on emerging technologies and government missions.
cleanedText:
Related News: Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) Technology
- Kerala State Electricity Board (KSEB) and IIT Bombay have initiated a pilot project to explore the implementation of Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology.
- About V2G Technology:
- V2G refers to technologies that enable EV batteries to send power back to the grid.
- When an EV is not in use, it can act as a decentralised battery energy storage device.
- By incorporating V2G technologies, EVs offer an opportunity to facilitate the integration of Renewable Energy (RE) and support a demand response market.
- It can also help modulate power in an EV battery to reduce the impact of variable RE on the grid and improve grid stability.
8.6. HYPERLOOP MOBILITY SYSTEM
Why in the News?
Maharashtra inks deal with IIT Madras startup (TuTr Hyperloop Pvt Ltd) to build hyperloop.
More on the News
- The proposed Linear Induction Motor (LIM)-based hyperloop mobility system will connect Jawaharlal Nehru Port Trust in Navi Mumbai to the upcoming Vadhavan Port.
About Hyperloop Mobility System
- Concept: It is basically a magnetic levitation (maglev) system where pods travel at ultra-high speeds through low-pressure tubes.
- Its Functioning and Key Components:
- Hyperloop functions in a sealed tube with minimal air resistance, utilizing vacuums and magnetic levitation for hovering.
- Linear Induction Motors (LIM) propels pods silently enabling the theoretical speed of 1,200 km/h.
- Key components: Steel tubes, pressurized capsules, a compressor for airflow, and air bearing suspension.
- Advantages: Ultra-high speeds (e.g., Mumbai-Pune in 25 mins), energy efficiency (potentially carbon-free), noise reduction, and logistics redefinition (moving cargo quickly and efficiently).
- Diagram Components: Hyperloop tube, Batteries, Seating, Transportation vehicle, levitation, Motor and generators.
8.7. DEEP OCEAN MISSION (DOM)
Why in the News?
Samudrayaan Mission will be launched by 2026 end as per the National Institute of Ocean Technology (NIOT).
More on the News
- Recently, Two Indian aquanauts dived to depths of 4,025 meters and 5,002 meters in the Atlantic Ocean, placing India among a select group of nations capable of such deep-sea exploration.
- The dives, conducted aboard the French submersible Nautile in collaboration with IFREMER, France's marine research institute, were led by team from NIOT, Chennai.
- The dive is a prelude to India's Samudrayaan Mission.
- So far, only the U.S., Russia, China, France and Japan have carried out successful deep-ocean crewed missions.
